items.title: As soaring temperatures prompt public health warnings in Peel, the cost to stay cool may be more than some rental tenants can afford
items.summary: Advocacy group ACORN recently surveyed more than 700 tenants and found 44 per cent said they did not have access to air conditioning.

items.title: Air quality warnings prompt outdoor pool closures, other measures in Mississauga
items.summary: Mississauga says it will continue to monitor weather patterns and federal air quality alerts before deciding when to reopen facilities.

City closes outdoor pools and adjusts recreation programs due to poor air quality
items.summary: Due to wildfires in other parts of Ontario, Mississauga continues to experience poor air quality and a Very High (10+) Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) . Based on this rating, residents are advised to avoid strenuous outdoor activities. If you must be outdoors, it is advised to minimize smoke exposure as much as possible, and prioritize keeping cool. Children, older adults, and individuals experiencing symptoms such as coughing or throat irritation should take extra precautions and limit outdoor physical exertion.

items.title: Poll: 80% of respondents won’t vote for Bonnie Crombie for mayor of Mississauga
Mississauga’s air quality affected by wildfire smoke
items.summary: Smoke from forest fires is expected to impact air quality in Mississauga, with conditions that may persist through the day. As smoke levels rise, so do health risks — making it important for residents to stay informed and take steps to protect themselves. Wildfire smoke can travel long distances and differs from typical urban air pollution. It contains fine particles and harmful gases that can enter the lungs and bloodstream, potentially causing inflammation and affecting overall health.

items.title: 2 dead, 3 injured in Highway 401 crash in Mississauga, OPP says
